head: linear probe

site_scanner.detail: scanner predicted as well as outcome (margin -0.39); likely acquisition artifact

replication.detail: no site/cohort grouping to split on

caveats: Biomarker anchor unavailable (no plasma p-tau217/GFAP coverage): claim cannot be promoted until molecularly anchored.
Completeness: 4/5 gauntlet tests could not run; score is renormalized over the tests that did.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
There is no usable plasma p-tau217 / GFAP anchor here, so the molecular gate is unmet — the finding rests on imaging alone.
Confirm the effective sample size per split — subject-disjoint cross-validation on a modest cohort leaves each fold thin.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

evidence_ledger.detail: scanner predicted as well as outcome (margin -0.39); likely acquisition artifact
no site/cohort grouping to split on

narration: The claim “x” lands at “fragile” (0/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.50 separating AD from CN) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It sits below the promotion floor, so the honest read is that acquisition, demographics or generic aging can plausibly account for it, and it should not consume a scientist's quarter without stronger evidence. Where it stops being true: that the scanner/site mix in any new cohort matches this one — the leakage margin is only -0.39 AUC, so a different scanner distribution could erase the effect. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Referee only runs the audit and issues the verdict.
